summary: Phase 2 SMIFS multi-agent orchestrator verified end-to-end. Backend pytest: 20/21 passed (95.2%). All 7 intent branches of /api/agent/turn produce the spec-compliant block payloads (KNOWLEDGE+citations including aif_overview; LEAD_CAPTURE with NCDs→NCD plural normalization in form.schema.context.asset_class; MARKET_DATA RELIANCE last_price=2842.55 change_pct=1.24; CALLBACK_REQUEST with form_type='callback'; CLIENT_LOOKUP without code returns text-only, with SMIFS001 returns client_card name='Aarav Mehta' verified=false; ESCALATION returns escalation_card; SMALL_TALK returns text only). Empty message → 422. SSE /api/agent/turn/stream emits multiple 'event: status' frames (each with step+label) followed by exactly one 'event: result' carrying full payload (session_id, blocks, citations, model, intent). /api/leads persists both lead_capture and callback documents with status='new' in MongoDB 'leads' collection (verified via direct pymongo query). /api/leads with unknown form_type → 400. Backward compat: /api/chat returns the legacy {session_id, reply, model, grounded, citations} shape with NO 'blocks' field, and the AIF reply contains 'AIF/alternative investment'. Conversations endpoint persists intent + blocks + citations on assistant messages. Phase 1 endpoints (/api/rag/search, /api/admin/reingest with X-Admin-Token, /api/docs) unchanged. Frontend: Header pill correctly renders 'Engine online · gpt-4o-mini · 53 chunks' (after cold-start health probe completes — takes 2-5s, see note). 3 suggestion chips render. KNOWLEDGE flow via suggestion-0: streaming-status appears with label 'Routing your question…', assistant message gets data-intent='KNOWLEDGE', citation chip renders, popover opens on click. MARKET via suggestion-1: market-card renders with RELIANCE / INR 2,842.55 / +1.24% green up-pill. LEAD_CAPTURE typed: streaming labels seen include 'Routing your question…' and 'Preparing your form…'; form-block renders with NCD tag and 5 fields (name, email, phone, city, investment_range); form submission → form-success-N 'Request received' state. CLIENT_LOOKUP typed: client-card with 'Aarav Mehta', 'SMIFS001', 'Identity not verified' pill. ESCALATION typed: escalation-card renders with 'Request a callback' CTA — clicking the CTA sends a callback message and the next assistant turn renders the callback form (form-callback-{name,phone,preferred_time,topic,submit}). Reset-thread clears messages and clears localStorage. Reload preserves session_id in localStorage but message list resets (rehydration is Phase 3, not yet — confirmed expected behavior).

minor.issue: Off-topic query 'What is the weather in Mumbai today?' is classified by the router as SMALL_TALK (confidence=1.0, rationale='casual inquiry about the weather with no advisory content') instead of the spec-expected KNOWLEDGE (with grounded=false) or ESCALATION. The reply itself is safe — it punts the user to an external weather source, returns no citations, and does NOT fabricate any SMIFS-specific facts — so the spirit of the spec (no fabrication, no false citations) is honored. This is a router-prompt deviation, not a fabrication bug. Consider tightening the SMALL_TALK definition in router.py to exclude factual non-advisory queries about external entities (weather, sports, news), routing them to KNOWLEDGE-with-grounded=false instead.

design_issues.screen: Header health pill on initial cold load
issues: Pill shows 'Checking engine…' for the duration of the /api/health round-trip (2–5s on cold start). This is acceptable but the placeholder state is long-lived enough that an automated test or a real user on a slow network might wonder if the engine is down. Consider a subtle skeleton-pulse animation on the pill while loading, or a 1s timeout that swaps to 'Connecting…' to signal liveness.

action_items: Optional: tighten router prompt in /app/backend/agents/router.py so that purely factual non-advisory queries about external entities (weather, sports scores, news) are classified as KNOWLEDGE (with grounded=false) instead of SMALL_TALK. The current behavior is safe but deviates from the spec.
critical_code_review_comments: orchestrator.py: _branch_lead_capture re-runs the RAG agent for the prose intro before attaching the form (per spec, intentional). The latency of LEAD_CAPTURE is therefore equal to a KNOWLEDGE turn (~1.5–2.5s). For high-traffic landing-page conversion this could be cached by intent+asset_class to drop p50.

orchestrator.py emit_status callback is invoked in-line on the same task — for very fast intents (SMALL_TALK) the status events may all flush in <100ms which can race against the SSE client's parser. The frontend handles this fine (we observed 'Routing your question…' even on slow turns) but consider adding a minimum 50ms gap between status events for visual smoothness.

router.py: SMALL_TALK classification is too broad — it captures any non-advisory message including factual questions about external facts (weather, news). Consider adding a negative example in the router prompt: 'A question asking for any external real-world fact (weather, sports, news, geopolitics) is NOT SMALL_TALK; classify as KNOWLEDGE so the RAG agent can return grounded=false.'
Chat.jsx: The health pill text 'Checking engine…' has no aria-live region — screen readers won't announce when the engine comes online. Add aria-live='polite' to the pill container.

Chat.jsx: msg-assistant-N uses the messages-array index (so values are 1, 3, 5, 7 — every odd integer). This is correct but tooling that assumes 1, 2, 3, 4 sequencing will be confused. Consider documenting or switching to a per-role counter (msg-assistant-0, msg-assistant-1, …).

Frontend still does not rehydrate /api/conversations/{sid} on mount — Phase 3 work item, but worth re-flagging now that the conversation document carries rich block payloads and would be lossless to restore.

form_agent.py: lead_capture_form normalizes plurals (NCDs→NCD) at parse time. Verified working. Consider extending to other plural asset classes ('AIFs'→'AIF', 'PMSs'→'PMS', 'mutual funds'→'mutual fund') to harden against user typing variations.
/api/leads endpoint accepts both 'lead_capture' and 'callback' but rejects unknown types with 400 — confirmed. Consider adding rate-limiting (currently unauthenticated and could be spammed).
test_credentials: X-Admin-Token: smifs-admin-2026 (verified). No user accounts in Phase 2 — client cards always return verified=false until Phase 3.
seed_data_creation: Tests created throwaway conversation sessions and TEST_-prefixed lead documents in MongoDB collections 'conversations' and 'leads'. Mock seed data (mock_clients × 5, mock_market × 10) is auto-seeded by mocks.seed_if_empty() on backend startup; verified SMIFS001→Aarav Mehta and RELIANCE→2842.55 records present.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suite is now /app/backend/tests/test_smifs_phase2.py (21 cases) + /app/backend/tests/test_smifs_chat.py (Phase 0+1, 19 cases). Run all with `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/results.xml`. Important: msg-assistant-N in the frontend uses the FULL messages-array index (so consecutive assistant msgs are at indices 1, 3, 5, 7…); use a helper that picks the max numeric suffix instead of incrementing N. Suggestion chips disappear after the first user message — the welcome card is replaced by the thread — so for the 2nd+ flows, type into chat-input instead of clicking suggestion-N. Health pill takes 2–5s to populate on cold load (it depends on /api/health which runs an LLM probe); wait until 'Checking engine…' is replaced before asserting on its content. The escalation-cta-N button sends a 'Please call me back…' message which itself triggers a CALLBACK_REQUEST form — verified working. Form testids follow the pattern form-{form_type}-{field_name} and form-{form_type}-submit; success state is form-success-N (uses message index N). The SSE endpoint emits comments starting with ':' as heartbeats — strip them in any custom SSE parser. Active LLM is gpt-4o-mini (returned as 'gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18' in agent/turn responses).
rca of the issue: The single backend-test failure is a router-classification issue, not a fabrication bug. /api/agent/turn for 'What is the weather in Mumbai today?' returns intent=SMALL_TALK with rationale='The message is a casual inquiry about the weather with no advisory content.' The text block content is safe ('I recommend checking a reliable weather website or app for the latest updates on Mumbai's weather. How may I assist you today?'), citations=[] (correct), no fabrication (correct). The deviation is purely in the intent label. Mitigation: add a few-shot negative example in router.py classify() prompt steering external-fact queries to KNOWLEDGE (which then naturally falls through to grounded=false because the RAG corpus has no weather data).
